questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.85]\draw[{Stealth}-{Stealth}] (-0.6,0)--(5.6,0);\foreach \x in {0,1,2,3,4,5} {\draw (\x,0)--(\x,-0.12); \node[below] at (\x,-0.14) {$\x$};}\fill (0,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (0,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.94) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,1.26) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.94) circle (2.2pt);\fill (3,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (3,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (5,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\node[below] at (2.5,-0.62) {Books borrowed in a week};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The dot plot shows the number of books each student borrowed in a week. How many students borrowed exactly $1$ book?

options.why: Reads the value $1$ on the axis instead of counting the dots above it.
Counts the dots above $2$ instead of above $1$.
Gives the total number of students rather than how many borrowed $1$ book.

questions.solution_text:
Count the dots stacked above $1$
$=4\text{ students}$

options.why: Reads the Cheese column instead of the Ham column.
Reads the BBQ column instead of the Ham column.
Rounds down to the nearest gridline ($4$) instead of reading the odd bar height of $5$.

questions.solution_text:
Read the height of the Ham column
$=5\text{ slices}$

options.why: Picks the tallest column (frequency $8$) rather than the size with frequency $5$.
Confuses the frequency value $5$ with the shoe size $5$ on the axis.
Reads the size with frequency $6$ instead of the size with frequency $5$.

questions.solution_text:
Find the column whose height is $5$
It is centred on shoe size $6$

questions.question_text:
Data: $33, 35, 35, 41, 48, 52$.
In a stem-and-leaf plot with the tens digit as the stem, which leaves are written beside the stem $3$?

options.why: Writes the whole values as leaves instead of just the final digit.
Drops the repeated leaf $5$ instead of listing every value.
Uses the leaves of the values in the $40$s and $50$s, not the $30$s.

questions.solution_text:
The values in the $30$s are $33, 35, 35$
Leaves beside stem $3$: $3\;5\;5$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.85]\draw[{Stealth}-{Stealth}] (-0.6,0)--(5.6,0);\foreach \x in {0,1,2,3,4,5} {\draw (\x,0)--(\x,-0.12); \node[below] at (\x,-0.14) {$\x$};}\fill (0,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (0,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,0.94) circle (2.2pt);\fill (1,1.26) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (2,0.94) circle (2.2pt);\fill (3,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\fill (3,0.62) circle (2.2pt);\fill (5,0.3) circle (2.2pt);\node[below] at (2.5,-0.62) {Books borrowed in a week};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The dot plot shows the number of books $12$ students borrowed in a week. How many students borrowed more than $2$ books?

options.why: Includes the $3$ students who borrowed exactly $2$ books; "more than $2$" excludes $2$.
Counts only the students who borrowed exactly $3$ books and omits the $5$-book reader.
Counts only the single student who borrowed $5$ books and omits the $3$-book readers.

questions.solution_text:
More than $2$ means $3$ or more books
$2\text{ students}+1\text{ student}$
$=3\text{ students}$
